title: Vectors are directed segments generalized
role: Expand the arrow idea from the line into the plane and space without breaking the original intuition.
prompt_core: Illustrate arrows living in two and three dimensions, all understood as directed segments that can be translated without changing their identity. A few arrows should share magnitude and direction in different locations to emphasize free-vector thinking. Clean spatial engineering aesthetic, subtle depth, no clutter.
caption: A vector is the old directed segment grown into richer space.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "13.1 Vectors are directed segments generalized".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Expand the arrow idea from the line into the plane and space without breaking the original intuition.

Layout intent:
H — Half-page conceptual plate

Style direction:
space-arrow plate

Core image brief:
Illustrate arrows living in two and three dimensions, all understood as directed segments that can be translated without changing their identity. A few arrows should share magnitude and direction in different locations to emphasize free-vector thinking. Clean spatial engineering aesthetic, subtle depth, no clutter.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
A vector is the old directed segment grown into richer space.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

title: Magnitude becomes a norm
role: Show length in higher dimensions as an invariant under rotations.
prompt_core: Create a spatial scene where a vector rotates through different orientations while a glowing sphere or circular shell of constant norm remains fixed. The image should teach that magnitude in higher dimensions is not guesswork but the invariant preserved by rotation. Elegant, high-clarity, restrained palette of steel and light.
caption: Norm is the higher-dimensional descendant of length.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. title: The dot product measures alignment
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Make the dot product feel like measured directional agreement, not a mysterious formula.
prompt_core: Show two vectors meeting at an angle, with one casting a projection onto the direction of the other, and a luminous overlap-like quantity indicating alignment. The image should connect angle, projection, and magnitude in one concise composition. Precise linework, calm spatial depth, overlay-friendly for angle marks or projection brackets.
caption: The dot product measures how much one vector points along another.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. title: Matrices are transformation machines
role: Embodied matrices as devices that stretch, shear, rotate, and reflect entire space.
prompt_core: Illustrate a simple grid or room being acted on by an invisible machine so that the whole space stretches, shears, or rotates. Include a familiar object like a book or cube so the deformation is felt bodily. The scene should make a matrix feel like an action on space, not a rectangle of numbers. Clean, engineering-grade, concept-first.
caption: Matrices are machines that act on entire spaces at once.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

source_key: 13.5 Historical note: why this language won
title: Historical note: why this language won
role: Show why vectors and matrices became irresistible across science and engineering.
prompt_core: Create a measured montage linking artillery or navigation vectors, engineering stress diagrams, computer graphics grids, and modern data or physics imagery, all unified by transformation language. The point is not breadth for its own sake but the recurring usefulness of a compact language for direction and change. Crisp, coherent, no clutter.
caption: This language won because it organizes many worlds at once.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

title: Thought experiments
role: Invite the learner to feel free vectors, alignment, and order-sensitive rotations.
prompt_core: Show a book or block being rotated around one axis and then another in alternate orders, plus two identical arrows moved to different places without changing identity. Add a tiny projection scene for alignment. Sparse and tactile, like a margin note asking the reader to try it with their own hands.
caption: Move the arrow, rotate the book, and let the structure announce itself.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students.
